_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: DNB equivalent in Plastic Surgery/ Reconstructive Surgery
clinical_indications: Areas of skin and tissue damage resulting from prolonged immobility, unconsciousness, or inability to sense pain (e.g., spinal cord injuries, non-ambulatory individuals, systemic disease like diabetes, intensive care, incapacitated through intoxication, patients lying in bed, sitting in a wheelchair, wearing a cast for a prolonged time, elderly population)
Skin under pressure where less fat restricts blood flow, leading to tissue breakdown and vulnerability to infections.
Bedsores in common areas: Gluteal region, Heels of the feet, shoulder blades, back of the head, posterior knee.
Stage I Ulcers: intact skin, non-blanchable erythema.
Stage II Ulcers: Partial thickness skin and tissue loss, shallow wounds.
Stage III Ulcers: Full thickness skin loss, open wounds with deeper tissue damage.
Stage IV Ulcers: Full thickness tissue loss, ulcers with exposed bone, tendon or muscle.

Reconstructive surgery for deep or hard-to-heal pressure ulcers, or both, involving removal of dead tissue from the wound and use of fat, muscle, and/or skin from other parts of the patient's body to fill the wound cavity.

common_queries: Clinical notes or clinical photograph submitted do not justify the need for the pressure sore surgery.
Clinical notes detailing events leading to pressure sores are missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Supporting reports or clinical photograph are missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Detailed indoor case papers or treatment given are missing at claim submission.
Detailed procedure/operative notes are missing at claim submission.
Post-procedure clinical photograph of the affected part is missing at claim submission.
Discharge summary or follow-up details report is missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, examination.
surgical_options: Primary wound closure: direct advancement of wound edges (directly or in layers).
Skin grafts: harvesting a thin piece of skin from a donor area to replace skin in the defect or denuded area.
Local random pattern flaps: moving local tissues around the wound, based on random pattern of blood supply, into the wound defect.
Regional flaps: Muscle or musculocutaneous flaps, fascial or fasciocutaneous flaps, perforator flaps.
Free flaps: raising a defined island of tissue with an artery and vein, surgically detaching and moving it to the wound site, then surgically anastomosing vessels to re-establish blood flow.
Tissue expansion: gradual increment and recruitment of tissue surrounding a pressure ulcer.
